characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Catchphrase: "All things were possible." Character Tic: Gives a lot of awkward little smiles. "Eureka!" Moment: Frequently gets them off of seemingly unrelated objects or statements. Tends to go completely silent and stare into the distance with a look of realisation on his face.He is, in fact, so well-known for them that at one point a conspirator in one of the mysteries he's investigating intentionallyplants herself in his lapto avert one. He is, in fact, so well-known for them that at one point a conspirator in one of the mysteries he's investigating intentionallyplants herself in his lapto avert one. Good with Numbers: Can tell a supermarket checkout is faulty by doing the arithmetic in his head.

Tends to go completely silent and stare into the distance with a look of realisation on his face.He is, in fact, so well-known for them that at one point a conspirator in one of the mysteries he's investigating intentionallyplants herself in his lapto avert one. He is, in fact, so well-known for them that at one point a conspirator in one of the mysteries he's investigating intentionallyplants herself in his lapto avert one. Good with Numbers: Can tell a supermarket checkout is faulty by doing the arithmetic in his head.

characters.name: Adam Klaus
characters.description: Bad Boss: His go-to solution to most of his problems is to dump them onto Jonathan. Butt-Monkey: Nothing ever seems to go right for him; he'ssuccessfulfor certain, but is repeatedly humiliated, ridiculed and disrespected. Granted, he tends to bring it on himself most of the time. The Casanova: Of the lecherous variety. He has a differentGirl of the Weekin every episode and there will inevitably be turmoil and drama. False Rape Accusation: Adam is subjected to this in "The Curious Tale of Mr Spearfish". Stage Magic: The up-front performer who charms the crowds and performs the tricks. Jonathan is the actual brains behind the operation and anytime Adam modifies or tries to create his own tricks it inevitably goes badly.

characters.name: Maddie Magellan
characters.description: Consummate Liar: She's very good at luring Johnathan into helping out on cases when he really doesn't want to get involved. In one specific instance, Johnathan serves her some food he has made, and she convincingly claims it is terrible. It's actually delicious, and she's just trying to knock him down a peg. (SeeInsufferable Geniusabove.) Drives Like Crazy: Almost spends more cumulative time on camera during driving scenes not looking at the road than she does looking at it, much to Jonathan's chagrin. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: She can be selfish, petty, manipulative and downright rude, but she's an investigative journalist who works to clear the names of the wrongly accused, and continues to do it despite it not earning her much money. Exemplified inThe Scented Roomwhere she initially ropes Jonathan into solving a mystery to get her hands on a £50,000 reward, but by the end asks instead that the money be put towards giving the clients' neglected son the love and attention he really needs. Karma Houdini: Repeatedly commits crimes (trespassing, impersonating a police officer, using a mobile phone while driving, wasting police time, etc) but invariably escapes punishment for them. Put on a Bus: After the end of the third season, she moves to the States for a while. She stays in contact with Jonathan for a while and even provides information relevant to one of his cases, but isn't seen again in person.

characters.name: Carla Borrego
characters.description: Amicable Exes: "Amicable" may be a stretch, but despite a brief relationship and an awkward break-up, she and Jonathan were able to work together reasonably well on her later show.

characters.name: Joey Ross
characters.description: Always Someone Better: Joey has such a reputation as a paranormal investigator that some even considered her superior to Jonathan, although he often had greater technical knowledge of how certain things could be carried out than she did. Glory Hound: Will happily claim the credit after Jonathan solves the case.

characters.name: Polly Creek
characters.description: Dark and Troubled Past: In a sense; some of her storylines saw Polly investigate what she thought were buried memories of her mother's affair or some traumatic loss, but it always turned out to be a tamer explanation, such as her burying carrots to feed her dead pet rabbit.

characters.name: Gideon Pryke
characters.description: Achievements in Ignorance: "Black Canary" has himactually arrest the real killerwhen he first shows up despite having zero evidence at all to the man's guilt. Big Damn Heroes: He manages to rescue Jonathan twice. Once in "Black Canary", where he shows up just as an angry man nearly throttles Jonathan, threatening to nick him and his girlfriend and once in "The Clue of the Savant's Thumb" in whichhe managed to switch out gasoline the murderers were intending to use with apple juice and then used the electronic on his wheelchair to subdue them. "Eureka!" Moment:In "Black Canary", it takes him fiddling with his desk lamp to realise thatthe murderer moved the desk containing a lamp in order for their main witness to not figure out how the trick was done."The Clue of the Savant's Thumb" has him notice that the victim's hand was bundled up in his pocket, unlike his usual tic of having only his thumb out.This is because the corpse seen was actually a dummy with the victim's head on and thus, the "hand" was bundled up in the pocket.
